setting.note: Two rural families, the Pickles and the Lambs, wash up together in a big rambling house in working-class inner Perth and share it for twenty years. The map runs from the Lambs' lost country home in the south-west, into the shared house and its shop in the western suburbs, out to the wartime city on Perth Water, and always back to the Swan River, where a boy drowns at the start and is made whole at the end.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Tim Winton, first published 1991. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Cloudstreet, Tim Winton (1991). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The green-brown of the Swan River, the water that runs through the whole book. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
